encounter.discharge_destination: Usual place of residence with safety-net advice

encounter_summary: Breastfeeding difficulty: A term newborn has difficulty sustaining attachment at the breast during the birth admission. Infant feeding assessment, positioning support, hand-expression plan and monitored feeds. Effective attachment demonstrated and a community feeding follow-up arranged.

passages.text: Live singleton born in hospital at term and admitted to the postnatal ward for newborn care.
A term newborn has difficulty sustaining attachment at the breast during the birth admission.
Clinically well newborn with normal observations, hydration and oral examination.
Clinical impression: Neonatal difficulty in feeding at breast.
Observed feed identifies shallow attachment and ineffective transfer without another acute illness.
Infant feeding assessment, positioning support, hand-expression plan and monitored feeds.
Effective attachment demonstrated and a community feeding follow-up arranged.
Synthetic record only. Review trigger reference 927; not for patient care.
